data.title: Director, Strategic Projects
data.raw_snippet:
**Our business landscape**

We’re an award-winning global communications company operating in nine countries across the Middle East, North Africa, and Southeast Asia. Our strategy is to become the region’s leading digital infrastructure provider. Ooredoo Group’s strategic vision is guided by five ke
data.jd_text:
**Our business landscape**

We’re an award-winning global communications company operating in nine countries across the Middle East, North Africa, and Southeast Asia. Our strategy is to become the region’s leading digital infrastructure provider. Ooredoo Group’s strategic vision is guided by five key pillars:

* **Value-Focused Portfolio:**
  Boosting asset returns by focusing on telco operations, towers, data centres, the sea cable business and fintech.

* **Strengthen the Core:**
  Optimally using deployed capital and maintain an appropriate cost structure.

* **Evolve the Core:**
  Monetising opportunities to generate new revenue streams via programmes focusing on analytics, digitalisation of operations, and partnerships with digital service providers.

* **People:**
  Building an engaged and empowered workforce through integrated learning programs and coaching and mentoring.

* **Excellence in Customer Experience:**
  Creating superior customer experiences.

From day one, every employee who joins our team becomes an integral part of our success journey. We offer you the chance to enhance your skills, advance your career, and maintain a healthy work-life balance. Empowering you to catapult your personal and professional growth. If you’re looking to challenge your growth potential, Ooredoo is the employer for you.

**Job overview**

* The Director, Strategic projects is responsible for leading the planning, execution, and governance of the strategy’s most critical cross-functional initiatives
* This role ensures that high-impact projects are delivered on time, within scope, and in alignment with the
* Group’s strategic and financial objectives. The position combines leadership in corporate development, strategic execution, and stakeholder management across business units, regions, and external partners
* The role ensures end-to-end ownership of strategic projects, from origination to execution and value realization, aligning with the Group’s growth, digital transformation, and shareholder objectives.
* The role together with the team member reporting to the role holder will oversee a wide range of projects and programs.
* One of the initial focus will be the key strategic initiative related to the expansions in scope, and territories of Ooredoo’s push into Data Centers as well AI? GPU as a service.
* While Syntys team is responsible for the day-to-day operation of these assets, the role holder will drive the expansion into new territories which is a key part of the strategy
* It will also include new strategic projects as approved as part of the updated strategy.

The role holder acts not only as a PMO or project manager but as the project owner, driving the actual content and delivery of the project and providing content expertise in the relevant field.

**Your impact on our goals**

**Strategic Program Leadership**

* Lead the planning, structuring, and delivery of complex, multi-stakeholder projects, including joint ventures, market entries, and major transformations.
* Define strategic objectives, milestones, and performance indicators in coordination with the
* Establish project governance frameworks, steering committees, and reporting standards.
* Drive decision-making through data-driven insights, structured analysis, and scenario planning.

**Provide direct support to the CEO and Board during major transaction and strategic processes**
.

**Drive the portfolio of strategic projects, partnerships JV and transactions related to Data Center, Ai infrastructure and GPU as a service and AI infrastructure including**

* Drive expansion in this area in particular expansion outside Ooredoo Group and its affiliates current footprint
* Drive the design, execution and implementation of relevant strategic initiatives related to this area.
* Negotiate strategic agreements and partnerships

**Cross-Functional Program Management**

* Coordinate workstreams across Finance, Legal, HR, Operations, IT, and Business Units.
* Identify interdependencies, risks, and bottlenecks, and implement mitigation strategies.
* Manage cross-border teams, consultants, and advisors to deliver outcomes efficiently.
* Ensure alignment of initiatives with budget, resource allocation, and strategic KPIs.
* Coordinate engagement across all operating companies

**Executive & Communication Board**

* Prepare executive-level presentations, Board papers, and investor communications related to strategic projects.
* Translate complex analyses into clear recommendations and progress updates.
* Lead stakeholder management with theCEO and Group Strategy in stakeholder engagement with shareholders, regulators, and partners.

**Team Leadership & Capability Building**

* Lead and mentor project managers and analysts
* Foster a culture of accountability, analytical excellence, and collaboration.
* Institutionalize best practices in project management and performance tracking.

**Experience and Qualifications**

* Prior experience of transformation and transaction planning and execution within Ooredoo
* Experience driving large scale transformation projects
* Experience at a Senior Level in a telecom operator in strategy or M&A team at a senior level (CXO or CX-1)
* 15 / 20 years of experience preferable in the Telco industry (not just technical)
* Knowledge and prior experience relating to DC and AI
* Experience in managing large scale transformation projects
* Experience in managing top management escalations and creating buy-in with stakeholders
* Multi disciplinary cross border program management
* Finance – business planning, valuation, accounting, tax, budgeting, AOP impact assessment, accounting separation
* Employee transfer requirements
* Organisation planning
* Scope specification for transaction advisors
* Contract drafting and negotiation
* Corporate Governance
* Transitional services arrangements
* Bachelor or Master degree – finance or business preferred

data.title: Sr. AGM Procurement & Commercial Partnerships Head
data.raw_snippet:
**About Commercial Bank Of Qatar**
Commercial Bank, founded in 1975 and headquartered in Doha, plays a vital role in Qatar’s economic development by offering a range of personal, business, government, international and investment services. We believe in empowering our employees, providing them with
data.jd_text:
**About Commercial Bank Of Qatar**
Commercial Bank, founded in 1975 and headquartered in Doha, plays a vital role in Qatar’s economic development by offering a range of personal, business, government, international and investment services. We believe in empowering our employees, providing them with opportunities for growth and professional development.
  
  
By Joining us, you’ll be part of a workplace culture that fosters innovation, supports work-life balance, and encourages you to reach your full potential.
  
  
Join us in shaping the future of banking!
  
  
**Job Summary**

* The role is responsible for defining and leading Commercial Bank of Qatar’s enterprise-wide procurement, commercial strategy, and strategic partnership agenda.
* The role will position procurement and commercial capability as a core enterprise-wide strategic lever for the Bank, driving cost efficiency, innovation, resilience, and value creation across all business and functional areas.
* Operating at the intersection of business, technology, and external ecosystems, the role will shape and deliver a future-fit, enterprise-wide commercial model for the Bank, leveraging supplier networks, partnerships, and market opportunities to enhance long-term competitive advantage.
* The role will play a key role in the Bank’s strategy and transformation agenda, contributing to enterprise-wide decision-making and value creation

**Central Topics**

* Enterprise-wide accountability across procurement, third-party commercial activity, and external partnerships
* Coverage across all business lines, technology, operations, and corporate functions
* Strategic ownership of the Bank’s enterprise-wide supplier ecosystem, partnerships, and external innovation channels
* Leadership of a Bank-wide agenda spanning procurement, commercial management, partnerships, and ecosystem strategy

****Key Responsibilties****
**Procurement & Strategic Sourcing**

* Define and lead the Bank’s enterprise-wide procurement strategy, covering all categories
* Establish best-in-class source-to-contract and category management frameworks over time
* Drive enterprise-wide value creation beyond cost, including demand management and specification optimisation
* Embed data-driven procurement and digital enablement across the Bank

**Cost Management & Supply Chain Optimisation**

* Lead enterprise-wide cost optimisation and efficiency programmes aligned to Bank priorities
* Develop a structured approach to total cost of ownership (TCO) and demand governance across the organisation
* Enhance supply chain resilience, performance, and operational efficiency across critical suppliers

**Commercial Management (Policy and Execution)**

* Define and implement enterprise-wide commercial policies, standards, and governance frameworks
* Elevate contracting excellence, negotiation capability, and commercial discipline across the Bank
* Ensure alignment between commercial strategy and broader enterprise objectives

**Strategic Vendor & Supplier Ecosystem Management**

* Establish a structured approach to enterprise-wide strategic vendor engagement and segmentation
* Build long-term, value-driven supplier relationships aligned to Bank priorities
* Drive innovation, performance, and transparency across key suppliers and partners

**Third-Party Risk Management**

* Oversee the evolution of the Bank’s enterprise-wide third-party risk management framework
* Ensure robust governance across vendor risk, partnering closely with Regulatory Compliance and Operational Risk / Resilience to enable effective enterprise-wide oversight
* Support alignment with regulatory expectations and internal risk frameworks

**Commercial Partnerships**

* Define and lead the Bank’s enterprise-wide commercial partnerships strategy
* Identify, structure, and activate partnerships that support growth, capability enhancement, and market differentiation
* Work closely with business leadership to originate and scale partnership opportunities over time

**FinTech and “NextTech” Partnerships**

At Commercial Bank, we don’t just offer careers, We shape futures by pioneering

in Qatar’s banking sector, blending

approach to redefine banking through

solutions.

data.title: Chief Revenue Officer
data.raw_snippet:
Job Description
  

Every large organization in Qatar is now making decisions about artificial intelligence, data governance, and operational resilience — and almost none of them can prove those decisions were made correctly. The documentation exists. The policies exist. What does not exist, in most
data.jd_text:
Job Description
  

Every large organization in Qatar is now making decisions about artificial intelligence, data governance, and operational resilience — and almost none of them can prove those decisions were made correctly. The documentation exists. The policies exist. What does not exist, in most cases, is an independently verifiable record that connects the decision to the framework it was supposed to follow, timestamps it, and makes it immutable. That gap between intent and evidence is where regulators, auditors, and boards are beginning to ask uncomfortable questions.

We built a consultancy and a technology platform around closing that gap. We advise organizations on strategic planning, risk management, AI governance, data privacy, and operational resilience — and we built a proprietary diagnostic platform that produces cryptographic proof of compliance decisions across multiple regulatory frameworks simultaneously. Not documentation. Proof. Deterministic, hash-chained, auditor-ready.

What we have not built is a commercial presence in Doha. That is this role.

**The firm**

Koru Impact LLC is a management consultancy, IT advisory, and regulatory technology firm registered in the Qatar Financial Centre (Registration No. 04642). We hold five permitted activity codes: strategic and operating model planning, risk management advisory, IT consultancy in AI and machine learning, IT consultancy in governance and data privacy, and customised software. These codes are not sector-specific. Our clients include — or will include — banks, insurers, energy companies, telecoms operators, government entities, healthcare institutions, and any organization where governance decisions carry regulatory or reputational consequence.

We are pre-revenue. Incorporated February 2026. A 91-institution pipeline spanning Qatar and the GCC, tiered by regulatory urgency and commercial accessibility. The CEO is based in Istanbul. The platform is built. The advisory thesis is tested. The pipeline is identified.

What is missing is the person in Doha who converts all of that into signed engagement letters.

**What the role requires**

This is a B2B commercial partnership, not salaried employment. You would contract with us through your own legal entity and invoice for your work. There is no employment relationship, no sponsorship, and no guaranteed monthly income.

The work itself: converting named institutional leads into scoping conversations, proposals, and closed engagements. The target organizations are large and mid-size entities in Qatar and the GCC — across sectors — that face governance, risk, AI, data privacy, or operational resilience obligations they cannot fully address with internal resources alone. Banks and financial institutions are a primary segment, but they are not the only one. Energy companies preparing for ISSB sustainability reporting, telecoms operators deploying AI across customer operations, government bodies building AI governance frameworks, and healthcare institutions managing sensitive data architectures all sit within the firm's advisory scope.

You would represent the firm in meetings with C-suite and senior leadership: Chief Compliance Officers, Chief Risk Officers, Chief Technology Officers, Chief Information Security Officers, Heads of Strategy, and their equivalents across sectors. You would diagnose governance gaps, position our advisory and platform services, and write proposals that close.

You would work from our registered office at Burj Doha on a regular basis. The relationships that produce advisory mandates in Qatar are built in person, in Arabic and English, with people who recognize your name before you arrive.

The revenue target is $200,000 in Year 1. The pipeline is built. The product is real. What is absent is a commercially credible principal who can sit across the table from a decision-maker at QNB, Ooredoo, Qatar Energy, or the Qatar Free Zones Authority and articulate why this firm's approach to governance is worth $25,000 to $200,000 of their budget.

**How you earn**

Three streams. All performance-linked.

*Delivery.*
When you deliver advisory work personally, you earn from the engagement delivery pool at your agreed hourly rate ($150–$250 depending on engagement complexity). The delivery pool is total engagement revenue minus the firm's platform fee. Deliver solo and the entire pool is yours.

*Origination and introduction.*
For every engagement you originate: a credit equal to 8% of the platform fee. For every new client you introduce: 15% of gross engagement value on the first engagement, 10% on the second, and a 36-month trailing credit of 2% on all subsequent work with that client.

*Revenue royalty — 60 months.*
You receive 3% of all collected firm revenue for five years, beginning from the date you close your first engagement. All firm revenue — not only engagements you originated. This is a platform participation right that compounds as the practice grows.

At $100,000 in Year 1 collected revenue with 50% personal delivery, total compensation is approximately $50,000. At $200,000 with 30% personal delivery, total compensation exceeds $63,000 with no ceiling above that. The five-year royalty alone is worth $30,000 at $200,000 annual revenue, scaling linearly with growth.

There is no retainer. No draw. No floor. This structure is designed for a person whose network, commercial instinct, and professional reputation are sufficient to generate revenue without a salary guaranteeing they will try.

**Who this is for**

You are currently resident in Doha with a valid Qatar ID. You have spent at least five years in a client-facing advisory, consulting, or business development role in the GCC — selling professional services, technology advisory, or management consulting to institutional buyers. Not products. Services. The distinction matters: you understand that a $75,000 advisory engagement is sold on trust, scoped on insight, and closed on credibility — not on a feature list.

You have closed B2B engagements worth $50,000 or more, repeatedly, with organizations that have procurement functions and budget committees. You are comfortable in a room with a CTO, a CCO, or a government undersecretary, and you can hold a conversation about governance, risk, data privacy, or AI strategy without reading from a script.

You speak business-fluent English. Arabic at business fluency or above is strongly preferred — it remains the primary language of institutional trust in Qatar.

You operate through your own legal entity, or you are prepared to establish one promptly.

**Who this is not for**

If you require guaranteed monthly compensation above QAR 25,000, this opportunity is structured differently than what you need. If your experience is exclusively in product sales — software licences, hardware, commodities — the consultative selling cycle in advisory work is a different discipline. If you have never closed a B2B professional services engagement above $50,000, the timeline does not accommodate a learning curve. If you are currently employed by a prospective client organization, the conflict-of-interest exposure is disqualifying for both parties.

**Next step**

Send a direct message to the Director ( Sener Cem Irmak) with two things: a brief note on why this interests you, and the names of three organizations in Qatar where you hold active senior relationships. No CV at this stage. We assess fit through conversation.

First conversations happen within 48 hours.

* Koru Impact LLC Qatar Financial Centre · Registration No. 04642 Burj Doha, Servcorp Qatar LLC · Doha, Qatar
* https://koruimpact.org

data.title: Director of Risk
data.raw_snippet:
About the Role

AlloyX is building a regulated, institution-grade stablecoin ecosystem through AX Coin. We are seeking a Director of Risk to lead the enterprise-wide risk framework, ensuring robust governance, regulatory compliance, capital and liquidity discipline, and effective risk oversight acro
data.jd_text:
About the Role

AlloyX is building a regulated, institution-grade stablecoin ecosystem through AX Coin. We are seeking a Director of Risk to lead the enterprise-wide risk framework, ensuring robust governance, regulatory compliance, capital and liquidity discipline, and effective risk oversight across all business activities.

This is a senior leadership role with direct engagement with the Board, regulators, and executive management.

⸻

Key Responsibilities

• Own and lead the enterprise-wide risk management framework, including risk appetite, risk limits, and risk governance.

• Establish and oversee prudential risk measures, methodologies, analytics, models, and reporting.

• Ensure strong alignment with regulatory standards (e.g. Basel III–style principles, capital adequacy, liquidity management, solvency concepts).

• Oversee key risk areas including credit, market, operational, liquidity, capital adequacy, and balance sheet risks.

• Lead stress testing frameworks, scenario analysis, and sensitivity testing.

• Manage asset–liability management (ALM), portfolio allocation, and reserve risk oversight.

• Oversee valuation practices across different asset classes and reserve instruments.

• Ensure accuracy and integrity of data flows, financial reporting, and risk disclosures.

• Produce and present risk reports for:

• Board and Board Committees

• Regulators and prudential authorities

• Annual reports and public disclosures

• Partner closely with Compliance, Finance, Treasury, and Technology teams.

• Build and lead a high-performing risk function, ensuring the right competencies, tools, and controls are in place.

⸻

Required Experience & Expertise

• 5–7+ years of experience within a risk management function in financial services, banking, payments, fintech, or regulated digital assets.

• Strong understanding of:

• Enterprise Risk Management (ERM)

• Capital allocation and liquidity management

• Prudential supervision and regulatory expectations

• Risk analytics, models, and reporting

• Hands-on experience dealing with regulators, auditors, and board-level stakeholders.

• Familiarity with portfolio management, ALM, reserve management, and financial risk controls.

• Deep understanding of the competencies and operating model required to manage a modern risk function.

⸻

Qualifications (One or More Preferred)

• Financial Risk Manager (FRM)

• Professional Risk Manager (PRM)

• Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA)

• Associate in Risk Management (ARM – The Institutes)

• Professional qualification in Actuarial Sciences

• Other equivalent risk or financial services qualifications

⸻

Why Join AlloyX / AX Coin

• Lead risk for a regulated, next-generation stablecoin platform

• Work closely with founders, board members, and regulators

• Shape the risk architecture of a high-growth digital financial institution

• Competitive compensation and leadership visibility

data.title: Field Medical Advisor Saudi
data.raw_snippet: BeOne continues to grow at a rapid pace with challenging and exciting opportunities for experienced professionals. When considering candidates, we look for scientific and business professionals who are highly motivated, collaborative, and most importantly, share our passionate interest in fighting c
data.jd_text:
BeOne continues to grow at a rapid pace with challenging and exciting opportunities for experienced professionals. When considering candidates, we look for scientific and business professionals who are highly motivated, collaborative, and most importantly, share our passionate interest in fighting cancer.
  
  
As a member of BeOne MENA organization, the Field Medical Advisor will support local strategies and establish compliant relationships with opinion leaders, research collaborators and key organizations in support of the BeOne pipeline development across all therapeutic areas.
  
  
This position will require pre- launch and post-launch Medical Affairs support for oncology pipeline development. The incumbent will work closely with internal colleagues in medical affairs, clinical development, program leadership, clinical operations, legal, compliance, new product planning, safety/pharmacovigilance, molecular diagnostics, translational research and other cross- functional business partners.
  
  
The Field Medical Advisor will support BeOne pre-clinical and clinical pipeline development by coordinating external collaborations, research endeavors with heath care providers and external academicians. He/She will play a key role in the establishment of BeOne as a valued collaborator and best-in-class biopharmaceutical company.
  
  
The position has a scope of Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
  
  
This position reports to the MENA Medical Director and will be based in Riyadh, KSA.
  
  
**Essential Functions Of The Job**

* Serve as BeOne expert in the assigned therapy area/s.
* Provide education to the medical communities on BeOne products and disease areas of focus.
* Work closely with internal stakeholders to develop and efficiently execute medical plans designed to meet shared objectives.
* Facilitate BeOne responses to unsolicited requests for medical information, often in direct collaboration with BeOne Medical Information Services personnel.
* Develop tactics for communicating complex scientific information to the healthcare communities.
* Provide impactful feedback/information to internal stakeholders on emerging clinical and scientific trends from external interactions to help shape company research, and to ensure the safe and appropriate use of BeOne products.
* Build specific excellence and integrity within the healthcare communities.
* Develop contacts with key clinicians, university teaching hospitals, group purchase organizations and other medical / scientific groups.
* Develop speakers through one-on-one clinical training on approved clinical slide sets.
* Act as a clinical resource with professional and advocacy organizations with the objective of developing relationships and identifying potential business opportunities.
* Assist in the development and execution of clinically relevant regional medical meetings (e.g. advisory boards, consultancy and investigator meetings), as requested
* Manage a significant volume of projects developed in a field-based environment, while demonstrating the ability to prioritize, excellence in execution.
* Optimizes the patient centricity of medical communications and deliverables, to ensure that patients remain the point of focus.
* To develop and maintain ongoing role model cross-functional working relationships with BeOne colleagues Locally, Regionally & through the Global organization.
* Identification and verification of investigators and sites for company-sponsored clinical trials
* Serve as the primary territory liaison for the submission and review of Investigator - Research proposals, facilitating the process from concept submission through publication and uncover scientific data that may have the potential for publication and/or abstract presentation.

**Experience**

* Existing experience in Medical Affairs within other biotech/pharmaceutical companies.
* Solid experience in Oncology is a must.
* Awareness of pharmaceutical industry codes, policies and local regulations.
* Medical Affairs experience in KSA is preferred.
* Minimum 2+ years of related experience is required

**Capabilities And Competencies**

* Good verbal and written communication skills.
* Possess strong analytical, interpersonal skills and self-awareness
* Ability to effectively collaborate in a dynamic local and global environment.
* Strong comprehension of disease states, and knowledge of differential algorithms for treatment decisions and experience in disease states of interest to BeOne, as well as the ability to communicate large amounts of scientific information in a clear and concise manner
* An expert understanding of the pharmaceutical industry, the country healthcare system, regional cancer care models and the impact of positive change on patient care
* Able to lead training initiatives and innovatively create best practices.
* Can be depended on to rapidly adopt and utilize new digital technology and other resources with medical customers and record medical interactions
* Strong time management and organizational skills.

**Supervisory Responsibilities**

* This role has no direct reports.

**Computer Skills**

* Proficient in Microsoft Office
* Experienced with electronic CRM systems

**Other Qualifications**

* High level of English communication (written and verbal)

**Travel:**
Work-related travel required within Saudi as well as participation in major congresses. This role is field based, 80% of the time required to be in the field.
  
  
Global Competencies
  
  
When we exhibit our values of Patients First, Driving Excellence, Bold Ingenuity, and Collaborative Spirit, through our twelve global competencies below, we help get more affordable medicines to more patients around the world.
  
  

* Fosters Teamwork
* Provides and Solicits Honest and Actionable Feedback
* Self-Awareness
* Acts Inclusively
* Demonstrates Initiative
* Entrepreneurial Mindset
* Continuous Learning
* Embraces Change
* Results-Oriented
* Analytical Thinking/Data Analysis
* Financial Excellence
* Communicates with Clarity

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. BeOne does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, national origin, veteran status or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

data.jd_text:
**Role –**
Lead Consultant
  
  
**Technology**
– ERP Finance Fusion
  
  
**Location**
– Muscat, Oman
  
  
**Business Unit**
– ORC
  
  
**Compensation –**
Competitive (including bonus)
  
  
**Job Description**
We are seeking a highly experienced Oracle Fusion Finance Lead/Principal Consultant to oversee and drive end ‑ to ‑ end implementation and support of key Fusion Financials modules, including General Ledger (GL), Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), Fixed Assets (FA), Cash Management (CM), Expense Management, Tax, Revenue Management Cloud Services (RMCS), Projects and Advanced Collections. The consultant will work closely with Finance leadership, business SMEs, and cross ‑ functional teams to define solution architecture, lead workshops, guide functional consultants, ensure seamless module integration, and provide expert oversight for project execution. This role requires strong command over financial processes, statutory and compliance requirements, and the ability to independently lead discussions with the customer.
  
  
**Your role**
In the role of a Lead or Principal consultant, you will serve to facilitate Finance Functional role with below responsibilities as followed.
  
  

* Lead end-to-end delivery of Oracle Fusion Finance modules: GL, AP, AR, FA, CM, Expense Management, Tax, Revenue Management Cloud Services (RMCS), Projects and Advanced Collections (if applicable).
* Conduct requirements workshops with customer business users and senior office, Finance Controllers, AP/AR teams, Treasury, and Audit teams.
* Translate business processes into future-state Fusion Finance architecture.
* Prepare solution designs, BRDs/MD50s, test scripts, and configuration documents

**Responsibilities**

* Module Configuration & Functional Expertise

+ Configure Fusion Financial modules based on global design principles and local statutory needs.
+ Manage Chart of Accounts (CoA), ledgers, legal entities, accounting rules, subledger configurations, and tax setups.
+ Support period close processes, reconciliations, journals, allocations, and intercompany flows.
+ Drive setup for bank accounts, cash pooling, payment formats, and treasury workflows.

* Finance Integrations & Reporting

+ Provide functional leadership for integrations with Payroll, Procurement & SCM, Treasury systems, Banking interfaces, and third-party invoicing platforms.
+ Collaborate with technical teams on FBDI/HDL-based loads, OIC/REST API integrations, and OTBI/BI Publisher reports.
+ Lead financial data validation, reconciliation, and cutover activities.

* Project Governance & Customer Engagement

+ Independently handle client discussions, workshops, and solution walkthroughs.
+ Partner with PMO on planning, RAIDs (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies), and status reporting.
+ Manage functional consultants working across different finance modules.
+ Ensure compliance with audit, internal controls, and statutory requirements .

* Testing, Rollout & Support

+ Lead SIT, UAT, regression testing, and hypercare support.
+ Drive quarter-end and year-end support readiness.
+ Own issue triaging, resolution management, and continuous improvements.
+ Guide business users on new features and quarterly Fusion Cloud updates .

* Required Skills & Experience

+ 10–15 years of experience in Oracle ERP implementations; 6+ years in Oracle Fusion Cloud.
+ Strong expertise in at least 4–5 Finance modules (GL, AP, AR, FA, CM, Taxes, Revenue Management Cloud Services (RMCS), Projects, Expenses).
+ Deep knowledge of Subledger Accounting (SLA), multi-ledger architecture, and cross-module flows.
+ Hands-on experience in finance data migration (FBDI uploads, opening balances, historical data, EBS to Cloud).
+ Experience with Oracle Cloud implementation methodologies
+ Experience in Telecommunications sector
+ Strong understanding of statutory reporting, localization, and period close processes.
+ Ability to lead functional teams and coordinate with technical architects.
+ Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

**Preferred**

* Should be excellent planner when it comes to perform release planning and other delivery planning.
* Should have excellent problem-solving skills
* Experience in GCC/Middle East finance regulatory environments.
* Exposure to enterprise multi-company, multi-country rollouts.
* Familiarity with integrations involving banks, treasury systems, and procurement.
* Experience in onsite–offshore delivery models.

**Personal**
Besides the professional qualifications of the candidates, we place great importance in addition to various forms personality profile. These include:
  
  

* Strong analytical ability and business acumen.
* Excellent leadership, communication, and consulting presence.
* Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
* High accountability and delivery focus.

**Why Infosys**
Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in more than 50 countries to navigate their digital transformation.
  
  
With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.
  
  
“All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.”

Acts as the primary point of customer interaction in the branch, promoting FAB products and services, supporting sales growth, and enhancing customer satisfaction.
  
  
Sales & Business Development

* Generate product leads from walk-in customers, referrals, and personal outreach.

Service Excellence & KPI Delivery

* Net Promoter Score (NPS) through positive and professional customer engagement.
* Complaint resolution timelines for customer service issues within SLA.
* Service Request (SR) turnaround times in line with agreed standards.
* Mystery Shopping scores through consistent delivery of FAB service standards.
* Handle customer queries, requests, and complaints professionally, escalating where necessary.
* Educate customers on FAB’s digital channels and self-service options.

Product Onboarding & Transactions

Operational Discipline & Compliance

* Treat compliance as a mandatory requirement, not a KPI.

**Healthcare Support Representative**

United Arab Emirates
  
  
**Reports to:**
Dealer Market Manager, UAE
  
  
**Role Purpose**
The Healthcare Support Representative is a UAE‑based role responsible for supporting the coordination and execution of healthcare projects across the UAE while providing operational support to dealer partners across the GCC region. The position is primarily operational, with approximately 70–80% of the role focused on coordination, documentation, and internal follow‑up, and 20–30% supporting dealer and client interactions related to healthcare opportunities and projects.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**
**Project Coordination & Operational Support**
Support the coordination of healthcare projects and opportunities by maintaining organized project information, tracking key activities, and ensuring timely follow‑up on requests and action items. Assist the Healthcare team in preparing project documentation, proposals, and internal updates to support effective decision‑making and project execution.
  
  
Dealer & Stakeholder Coordination
  
  
Act as a coordination point between MillerKnoll Healthcare and dealer partners across the UAE and GCC. Facilitate clear communication, dealer training, timely information exchange, and alignment between dealer partners, internal teams, and other stakeholders involved in healthcare opportunities.
  
  
Team & Client Support
  
  
Assist the Healthcare team in preparing for client meetings, product presentations, and healthcare facility visits. Support interactions with dealer partners and healthcare clients when required, ensuring project discussions, information requests, and follow‑up actions are clearly documented and communicated.
  
  
**Communication & Responsiveness Expectations**
Maintain consistent responsiveness during working hours and ensure timely communication with internal teams and dealer partners. Provide clear updates on project status, follow‑up actions, and any issues requiring attention.
  
  
**Minimum Requirements**

* University degree completed or in progress
* Strong communication skills in English and Arabic
* Strong organizational and time management skills

* Project coordination

* Facility management coordination
* Construction or fit‑out project support

**Personal Competencies**

* Highly organized and detail‑oriented
* Strong sense of accountability and ownership
* Proactive and responsive in communication

* Professional, reliable, and adaptable in a dynamic project environment

**Who We Hire?**
Simply put, we hire qualified applicants representing a wide range of backgrounds and abilities. MillerKnoll is comprised of people of all abilities, gender identities and expressions, ages, ethnicities, sexual orientations, veterans from every branch of military service, and more. Here, you can bring your whole self to work. We’re committed to equal opportunity employment, including veterans and people with disabilities.
  
  
MillerKnoll complies with applicable disability laws and makes reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact MillerKnoll Talent Acquisition at careers\_help@millerknoll.com.

data.raw_snippet:
**Job Purpose**

A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew.

As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you
data.search_title: "Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi" ("Chief Technology Officer" OR CTO OR "Chief Information Officer" OR CIO OR "Head of Technology" OR "IT Director" OR "Head of IT" OR "Chief Digital Officer" OR CDO OR "VP Digital Transformation" OR "Head of Business Excellence" OR PMO OR transformation OR director)
data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose**

A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew.

As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you need to be friendly, observant and able to provide the right support.

Being a member of the cabin crew is so much more than a service role - safety is our highest priority. You’ll need to lead confidently and take control when it comes to managing aircraft services, security, and safety procedures. This comes from the world-class learning experience our crew receive at our state-of-the-art facility in Dubai.

**Qualifications & Experience**

**Here are some other things we look for in our cabin crew:**

* You’ve had more than a year’s experience in hospitality/customer service
* You have a positive attitude and the natural ability to provide excellent service in a team environment, dealing with people from many cultures
* Your minimum qualification is high school graduate (Grade 12)
* You need to be fluent in written and spoken English
* You’re at least 160cm tall and can reach 212 cm while standing on tiptoes, to enable you to reach emergency equipment on all aircraft types
* No visible tattoos while you’re in Emirates cabin crew uniform (without covering them with bandages or cosmetics)
* As Emirates cabin crew, you’ll be based in Dubai and will need to meet the UAE's employment visa requirements

Aside from the requirements of the role, you should be determined to always perform to the highest standards, focus on being solution oriented, and be able to manage a demanding work schedule. You should have the ability to deliver an authentic experience to our customers. You’ll be culturally aware and reflect the Emirates personality - professional, empathetic, progressive, visionary and cosmopolitan.

Before clicking the Apply button, please keep the following
**documents**
ready to submit with your application:

* Recent CV in English
* Recent photo

**Salary & Benefits**

Your pay is made up of three components:

1. a fixed basic salary,
2. an hourly pay for operated flights and
3. an overseas meal allowance.

Basic salary = AED 4,980 / month,

Flying Pay = AED 69.60 / hour based on avg. 80-100 hours / month,

Average Total Pay = AED 11,244 / month (~USD 3,100, EUR 2,600 or GBP 2,280)

\* These are approximate numbers for Grade II (Economy Class).

Meal allowances for night stops are credited to the salary in arrears the following month.

Hotel accommodation as well as transport to and from the airport is provided by the company.

data.raw_snippet:
**Job Purpose**
Acts as the airline’s ambassador; strives to achieve customers’ loyalty and to promote a positive corporate image, at all times, through enhancing the overall customers’ experience. Provides excellent customer service to all passengers whilst ensuring their safety and comfort are ach
data.search_title: "Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi" ("Chief Technology Officer" OR CTO OR "Chief Information Officer" OR CIO OR "Head of Technology" OR "IT Director" OR "Head of IT" OR "Chief Digital Officer" OR CDO OR "VP Digital Transformation" OR "Head of Business Excellence" OR PMO OR transformation OR director)
data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose**
Acts as the airline’s ambassador; strives to achieve customers’ loyalty and to promote a positive corporate image, at all times, through enhancing the overall customers’ experience. Provides excellent customer service to all passengers whilst ensuring their safety and comfort are achieved throughout the complete flight cycle. Be ready and fit to operate ensuring productivity is in line with set measures and company’s adopted policies and procedures.
  
  
**Conduct**
**Key Result Responsibilities**

* Ensures timely attendance, proper grooming, fitness and legality of self for the assigned flights. Attends “Briefing” to ensure acknowledgement of flight details, distribution of roles, targets, security checks, boarding, etc.

**Safety Measures**

* Instructs passengers on safety procedures to be followed ensuring their safety and comfort at all times.
* Identifies areas of safety and security risks and partners with the Cabin Supervisor to take remedial action in accordance with the SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures).
* Administers first aid to passengers where necessary.
* Informs Cabin Supervisor, Flight Crew and Captain, as appropriate, with any irregularities and malfunctions promptly.

**Customer Service**

* Welcomes passengers and assists in their boarding formalities; enters necessary data into the aircraft logbook as per the Airline and Civil Aviation Authority’s adopted procedures.
* Provides constant excellent customer services to passengers by attending to their individual pre-bookings, requests and needs.
* Identifies and assists passengers experiencing stress or difficulties in accordance with the corresponding policies and procedures detailed in the Operations Manual and other relevant instructions.
* Contributes to maximizing on-board revenue and “Charity Cloud” program by efficiently promoting the on-board meals, products and charity services and thereby achieves individual and team targets.
* Ensures proper and secure handling of cash and credit card payments received against individual sales and donations managed by him/her.

**Crosschecking And Reports**

* Carries out pre-flight checks to ensure sufficient supplies, emergency equipment, catering equipment and other specified equipment prior to take off; provides report for any deficiencies to Cabin Supervisor and/or Captain as appropriate.
* Assists in ensuring the aircraft cabin is cleaned thoroughly at transit stations and that adequate re-catering and duty free products are provided before flying.
* Ensures orderly and complete handover/takeover of the cabin to/from catering, cleaning and/or maintenance staff.
* Carries out the post-flight briefing and assessment reports as per adopted policies and procedures: provides objective evaluation of the Cabin Supervisor, reports any problems or discrepancies on flight, closing and sealing of duty free containers.
* Accounts to Cabin Supervisor for the conduct of passengers; carries out the duties of ‘Cabin Supervisor’ when designated as such by the Captain.
* Maintains necessary professional and safety knowledge and competitiveness by attending recurrent trainings, emergency procedures trainings, and educational workshops and by ensuring current license.
* Contributes to team effort by accomplishing related results and achieving allocated sales targets and objectives.

**Qualifications (Academic, Training, Languages)**

* Diploma or Higher Secondary Certificate is acceptable for this role; a diploma in Travel/ Tourism is a plus.
* Training in First Aid, Safety and Emergency Procedures are an added value.
* Fit to fly, has good general health, no serious medical conditions or chronic diseases.
* Presentable, friendly, demonstrates cheerful and positive attitude even under stress.
* Customer service oriented, capable of understanding and attending to customers’ needs and requests.
* Capable of handling difficult situations and angry customers without compromising corporate image.
* Focused, capable of identifying irregularities and handling threats and emergencies with efficiency and commitment to successful results.
* Proactive, self-motivated, demonstrates initiative and positive teamwork competency.
* Very good verbal and written communication skills.
* Excellent time management and problem-solving skills.
* Proficient in Microsoft Office.
* Fluent in English Language. Other languages are an advantage based on the Airline requirements

**Important Requirements**

* Age: Between 20 29 for non-experienced crew, and up to 33 years for experienced crew
* Minimum Height: 160 cm (Female), 170 cm (Male)
* Weight: To be in proportion to Age and Height
* Clear skin with no marks or tattoos that would be visible whilst wearing the cabin crew uniform
* Medically/ Physically fit to perform Cabin Crew duties
* Ability to swim with aid of a flotation device

**Work Experience**

* Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply for this role.
* Previous experience as cabin crew or in a customer service role in the Airlines/Aviation/ or hospitality Industries shall be treated as an added value.
* Active cabin crew license is an advantage.
* Capable of working in shift pattern and for extended working hours.
* Shows high flexibility and adaptability to frequent changes and busy schedules.
* Demonstrates the ability to contribute and successfully deliver against policies, procedures, and set KPIs.

data.search_title: "Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi" ("Chief Technology Officer" OR CTO OR "Chief Information Officer" OR CIO OR "Head of Technology" OR "IT Director" OR "Head of IT" OR "Chief Digital Officer" OR CDO OR "VP Digital Transformation" OR "Head of Business Excellence" OR PMO OR transformation OR director)
data.jd_text:
Job Purpose

We are seeking a highly skilled Power Apps Developer to design, develop, and implement enterprise-level solutions using Microsoft Power Platform. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience with Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse, and integrations with Microsoft 365 and Azure services. This role requires strong problem-solving skills, and experience delivering scalable business applications.

Principal Accountabilities

**General**

* Should be able to work independently as well as in team. Must possess good analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Should be able to effectively communicate (verbal or in written) with Customers, team members and Product vendors on the matters related to all functional or technical areas
* Documents and maintain Knowledge database to share experience and knowledge with team members in order to enhance efficiency and reduce single person dependence
* Coordinate work tasks with Customer, team members, and Product Vendors to perform the tasks assigned efficiently in Timely manner
* Analyses business needs and conceptualises solutions to meet and exceed customer expectations
* Should be Customer centric, achieve customer satisfaction and maintain proper perspective in all activities.

**Operational**

* Design, develop, and deploy Canvas and Model-Driven apps using Microsoft Power Apps
* Build and manage automated workflows using Microsoft Power Automate
* Design and manage data models within Microsoft Dataverse
* Integrate Power Platform solutions with:
* **Microsoft 365**
* **Microsoft Dynamics 365**
* **Microsoft Azure**
* **Third-party systems via APIs and custom connectors**
* Develop custom components using Power Apps Component Framework (PCF), and Power Apps plugins.
* Implement role-based security and governance best practices
* Optimize app performance, scalability, and maintainability
* Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions
* Support DevOps practices including solution packaging and deployment pipelines
* Troubleshoot and resolve production issues
* Attention to detail and commitment to high-quality, error-free deliverables
* Should have good understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)
* Good communication skills suitable for liaison with departmental staff and business stakeholders
* Ability to produce technical documentation and participate in technical discussions
* Must be motivated, independent, and self-sufficient; able to receive an assigned task and see it through to completion with minimal supervision.

**Education**

* Degree: Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or equivalent
* Microsoft Power Platform certifications (PL-400, PL-600) are a plus.

**Experience**

* 5+ years of experience in application development
* 3+ years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Power Platform
* Strong knowledge of:
* Canvas Apps & Model-Driven Apps
* Power Automate
* Dataverse data modeling
* REST APIs & custom connectors
* JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, .NET
* Experience integrating with Azure services (Azure Functions, Logic Apps, Service Bus)
* Strong understanding of ALM (Application Lifecycle Management)
* Experience with DevOps and CI/CD pipelines.
* Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
* Knowledge of Agile/Scrum methodologies.
* Experience with Power BI integration is a plus.
* Experience with Copilot Studio, AI Builder, and RPA are a plus.

data.search_title: "Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi" ("Chief Technology Officer" OR CTO OR "Chief Information Officer" OR CIO OR "Head of Technology" OR "IT Director" OR "Head of IT" OR "Chief Digital Officer" OR CDO OR "VP Digital Transformation" OR "Head of Business Excellence" OR PMO OR transformation OR director)
data.jd_text:
**Role Overview**
HR Officer provides HR advice and support to COO in line with the company HR policy. Source and recruit the right quality of manpower, within agreed timeframe in order to meet the manpower requirements of the organisation. Maintain up to date information on HR activities and processes in order to provide analysis and reporting. To be responsible for the provision of an efficient and effective payroll and incentive service, to provide employees and management with accurate information at all times.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Payroll: Ensure employees are WPS compliant. Collecting, calculating, and entering data in order to maintain and update payroll information, ensuring all payroll and incentive transactions are processed efficiently. Answering employee questions and complaints about payroll. Maintain high confidentiality of all HR & payroll-related information and records
* Operational: Preparing NOC's, salary certificates, experience letter, warning letters, terminations letters, final settlement acknowledgment letter and other employee requests. Prepare daily attendance report, weekly new joiners report, weekly open position report. Issuing Increment and promotion letters based on performance assessment.
* Insurance: Assist Head of HR & Management in the confirmation of the yearly Insurance policies. Addition & Deletion of Employees based on joining and exit respectively. Act as liaison between the staff and Insurance for medical claims and any other information.
* Recruitment: Design and update job descriptions, sourcing, screening and shortlisting profiles through online as well as from the recruitment partners. Conducting preliminary interviews via phone, video and in person. Scheduling and inviting candidates for interview with concerned department heads. Issuing offer letter and employment contract to selected candidates with management approval. Provide feedback to unsuccessful candidates. Provide weekly reports to the operations and management. Ensure arrival of new employees on time and coordinate with PRO to complete visa process
* Visa Process/Employee Documentations: Provide PRO with all necessary information with regards to new joiners. Follow up on the submitted visa applications from the PRO. Maintain accurate and up to date employee files, records and ensure safe storage.
* On boarding: Ensure all new hires are provided with an onboarding program including the company property form, joining report, bank account information, employee data information sheet and employee handbook. Ensure all the new joiners go through HR induction and NDA's
* Exit Management: Coordinating and conducting Exit Interviews. Tracking of exiting employees on the status of their final settlements and visa cancellations. Resolve issues related of exiting employees pertaining to timesheet submission and settlement released.
* Training: Responsible to book the training (Occupational health card, basic food hygiene, firefighting certificate and basic first aid) as per business requirements.
* Employee Grievances: Support managers and employees through the grievance and disciplinary process to ensure efficient and satisfactory resolutions in line with HR policies and procedures. Resolving employee conflicts/issues related to leaves, salaries, final dues and exit process.
* Confidentiality: Handle sensitive and confidential information with the utmost discretion and professionalism.

**Education/Qualification**

* Master's degree
* HR specific qualification – desirable
* Minimum 3 to 5 years HR experience
* Recruitment experience is a must
* HRMS experience, preferably Bayzat.
* Understanding of relevant Labour Laws
* Problem solving
* Planning and organising
* Excellent verbal and written communication
* Proficient in computer applications

data.jd_text:
**About Edenred**
Edenred is the leading digital platform for corporate services in the areas of benefits, professional mobility, and B2B payments.
  
  
Operating in 44 countries, it connects more than 60 million users to over 2 million partner merchants through more than 1 million client companies.
  
  
Edenred offers solutions dedicated to employee engagement (meal vouchers, commuting, gift vouchers, wellness, rewards, and preferential offers to boost purchasing power), mobility (multi-energy solutions, including EV charging, fleet management services, maintenance, toll, and parking), and corporate payments (virtual cards). In addition to its own solutions, Edenred also distributes offers from more than 120 partners on its platform.
  
  
True to the Group's purpose, "Enrich connections. For good.", these solutions enhance users' well-being and purchasing power and simplify the lives of professional drivers while promoting access to healthier food, more environmentally friendly products, and more sustainable mobility. They improve the attractiveness and efficiency of businesses and vitalize the employment market and the local economy.
  
  
Edenred’s 12,000 employees are committed to making the world of work a connected ecosystem that is safer, more efficient and more responsible every day.
  
  
In 2024, thanks to its global technology assets, the Group generated a business volume of nearly €45 billion, mainly through mobile applications, online platforms and cards.
  
  
Edenred is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange and included in the following indices: CAC Next 20, CAC Large 60, Euronext 100, Euronext Tech Leaders, FTSE4Good, DJSI Europe Index, DJSI World Index, and MSCI Europe.
  
  
**THE UAE CONTEXT**
Edenred UAE is a fast growing payroll service provider serving more than 15,000 business and more than 2.2 million unbanked employees. We service over 10 banks and 35 financial institutions. We offer a unique value proposition for companies to manage their payroll and for employees to receive their salaries and use it in the simplest way offering multiple value added services via our mobile app (money transfer, mobile recharge and card services)
  
  
**THE ROLE**
This role involves managing the end-to-end client onboarding journey while ensuring full compliance with regulatory requirements and internal standards. This role also offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams to streamline processes, mitigate risk, and enhance onboarding efficiency. It is an exciting opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys solving compliance challenges, and wants to take ownership of impactful initiatives such as AML audits and process improvements. The position offers exposure to regulatory frameworks and business operations, making it ideal for a candidate looking to grow their career in compliance while contributing to a robust onboarding function.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Oversee the complete client onboarding lifecycle—from initial contact and documentation collection to compliance verification (KYB, KYC, AML, due diligence) and successful account activation.
* Conduct screenings of trade names, beneficial owners, and authorized signatories using internal screening tools, and perform periodic reviews for low, medium, and high-risk profiles to ensure ongoing compliance with regulatory and internal requirements.
* Collaborate effectively with internal departments including Sales, Compliance, and Operations to ensure a seamless onboarding experience while maintaining accurate and up‑to‑date records in systems and trackers.
* Manage multiple onboarding cases simultaneously, and consistently meeting deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
* Execute annual AML audits of business partners ensuring adherence to applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations and manage partner assessments, ensuring timely submissions.
* Review, update, and maintain internal SOPs and compliance documentation, ensuring all processes remain up to date.
* Oversee internal processes and drive mandatory AML training programs, managing compliance tracking/follow-ups, and escalation of non-compliance.
* Provide compliance support to internal teams on compliance requirements and conduct comprehensive reviews of internal processes.

**YOUR PROFILE**

* Strong documentation and writing skills with the ability to create and maintain structured processes and SOPs.
* Highly organized with excellent time management skills and attention to detail.
* Proactive mindset with a track record of initiating follow-ups, tracking progress, and ensuring timely completion of deliverables.
* Strong communication skills with a persuasive and collaborative approach across business functions.

Reliable and able to take full ownership and accountability for assigned projects.
  
  
**EDUCATION**

* Bachelor’s degree is mandatory, preferably in Business, Finance, Risk Management, or a related field.

**Experience**

* Minimum 3 years of experience within Compliance or Risk functions, with hands-on experience in client onboarding, KYC, CDD, EDD. Experience in a bank, fintech, payments, or regulated financial services environment is strongly preferred.

**CERTIFICATIONS (Preferred But Not Mandatory)**

* CAMS – Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist, ICA Certifications.

**COLLABORATION:**
The candidate will collaborate directly with Compliance Onboarding Team in charge of processes linked to onboarding of clients, as well as AML Compliance Team responsible for cross-functional AML compliance projects.

data.search_title: "Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi" ("Chief Technology Officer" OR CTO OR "Chief Information Officer" OR CIO OR "Head of Technology" OR "IT Director" OR "Head of IT" OR "Chief Digital Officer" OR CDO OR "VP Digital Transformation" OR "Head of Business Excellence" OR PMO OR transformation OR director)
data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose**
The Emirates Group is a highly profitable business comprising of Emirates Airline, the largest international airline in the world and dnata, one of the world's biggest air services providers. With our headquarters located in the cosmopolitan emirate of Dubai, working with us puts you at the forefront of technological advances in the aviation industry while offering you a dynamic lifestyle.
  
  
If you're seeking a position that will allow you to thrive professionally and develop your career in aviation industry, look no further. There are openings for individuals who are passionate about traveling and enjoy collaborating with customers and colleagues across the world.
  
  
We are looking for exceptional individuals who can be a part of our Customer Service team. If successful, you will join an extensive training program which include systems training and customer service skills and then be allocated to an area across the group: Emirates Airport Services, dnata, Marhaba services and Contact Centre.
  
  
**During The Hiring Process, You Can Expect**
**Step 1 – Apply**
Submit your application and we’ll then send you an email to confirm that we’ve received your application.
  
  
**Step 2 – Review**
Our Recruitment Team will review your application.
  
  
**Step 3 – Video Interview and Assessment**
If you’re shortlisted for the role, we’ll send you a link to our on-demand, interactive and gamified video interview assessment via HireVue. This is most critical part of the process and required if you want to progress to the next stage.
  
  
**Step 4 – Offer**
If successful, we’ll send you an offer of employment which will include the benefits package for your role.
  
  
We have outlined below the specific job details for each of our roles:
  
  
**I. Customer-facing Roles**
Airport / Customer Service Agent (Emirates and Dnata)
  
  
As an Airport / Customer Services Agent, you will be one of the first welcoming faces our customers meet at Dubai International Airport, a leading global aviation hub. Every day brings the opportunity to guide travellers from around the globe through check-in, boarding, arrivals, and baggage services with confidence, care, and professionalism.
  
  
From assisting lounge customers to supporting families, unaccompanied minors, connecting passengers, and those needing extra assistance, you will play a key role in ensuring every journey feels seamless and stress-free.
  
  
In this role, you will gain strong operational expertise, develop your people and problem-solving skills, and build a career in a dynamic, fast-paced, and highly international environment.
  
  
Strong performance in this role can also unlock opportunities for career progression and internal moves across airport operations, customer services, and wider business areas.
  
  
Marhaba Service Agent
  
  
As a Marhaba Services Agent, you will deliver a personalized airport experience to our guests, supporting them across key touchpoints including check-in, immigration, boarding and baggage services. You will provide dedicated assistance to VIP customers, families, unaccompanied minors and passengers requiring additional support. This role allows you to build strong customer engagement and service excellence skills, while offering career growth across Marhaba’s services, airport operations, and customer experience functions.
  
  
**II. Back-office Role**
Customer Sales and Service Agent – Contact Centre
  
  
As a Contact Centre Customer Sales and Service Agent, you will be the trusted voice of Emirates, delivering exceptional support and care throughout every stage of our customers’ journey. Your role is pivotal in creating seamless, reassuring experiences, whether you are helping customers plan their travel or resolving real-time queries with confidence and empathy. In this dynamic position, you will engage with customers across multiple communication channels, ensuring that every interaction reflects clarity, consistency, and up-to-date knowledge of our products and services. Your ability to actively listen, think critically, and communicate effectively will make customers feel valued and supported at all times.
  
  
Joining the Contact Centre fast-paced, multicultural environment means you will not only sharpen your communication, problem-solving, and sales expertise but also unlock clear pathways for career growth, both within the Contact Centre and across the wider Emirates Group.
  
  
Qualification
  
  
**To be considered for Customer Service roles, you must meet the following requirements:**

* Fluency in both spoken and written English
* Fluency in any other language is a plus
* A levels/High School certificate
* Excellent interpersonal and customer service skills
* Willing to work in a shift environment
* PC based skills to operate Windows package (Microsoft Word/Excel/E-mail)
* Willing to wear uniform (Front-line customer facing roles)

**In addition to the above, the job specific requirements for the Contact Centre role are:**

* Proficiency in English and at least one of the following languages is a must-have requirement for this position: Arabic, French, Czech, German, Italian, Japanese, Cantonese, Mandarin, Russian, Spanish, Polish, Portuguese.
* Customer and sales focus on providing excellent service and closing any sales opportunity.
* The ability to type accurately and quickly while maintaining excellent written communication is an essential requirement for the messaging-based role.

Experience the excitement of being on the front-line customer-facing roles with dnata, Marhaba and Emirates Airport Services Agents, or work behind the scenes as a Contact Centre Service Agent. You'll have the chance to work with a diverse team of professionals, interact with people from all over the world, and make a positive impact on the lives of travellers every day. Both full-time and part-time opportunities are available, providing flexibility to fit your lifestyle and career goals. Whether it be Emirates Airline or dnata, our goal is to ensure that our customers experience service is consistent with the Emirates Group high standards.
  
  
Salary & benefits
  
  
Join us in Dubai and enjoy attractive salary and travel benefits that are exclusive to our industry, including discounts on flights and hotels stays around the world.
  
  
Find out what it’s like to live and work in our fast-paced, cosmopolitan home city in the Dubai Lifestyle section of our website emiratesgroupcareers.com

data.jd_text:
Immerse yourself in the world of Cartier, where heritage and innovation come together to create unforgettable experiences.
  
  
With a growing presence across the UAE, we are looking for experienced Sales Associates to join us.
  
  
**HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?**
As a Sales Associate, you will create meaningful connections with clients, guiding them through their journey and sharing the unique craftsmanship behind each creation. You will play a pivotal role in achieving boutique goals by providing personalized advice, cultivating client relationships, and ensuring every interaction reflects the excellence of Cartier.
  
  
As an integral part of our Boutiques, you will be responsible for the following:
  
  
**Sales Achievements**

* Deliver exceptional client experiences in line with Cartier’s service excellence standards.
* Achieve and exceed individual and boutique sales objectives through consultative selling and deep product knowledge.
* Proactively identify and convert clienteling opportunities by scheduling appointments and developing robust client profiles.

**Client Relationship & Portfolio Development**

* Cultivate meaningful and lasting client relationships, fostering loyalty to the Maison.
* Strategically grow and manage your client portfolio to maximize engagement and sales potential.
* Execute targeted CRM actions to strengthen client connections and drive repeat business.
* Represent Cartier as a true Ambassador during client events and exclusive activations.

**Daily Boutique Operations**

* Uphold the highest standards of operational compliance across all boutique procedures and policies.
* Ensure accurate stock management, actively participate in inventory processes, and maintain the impeccable presentation of the boutique.

**One Team Spirit**

* Embody Cartier’s values and contribute to a collaborative, positive team spirit.
* Engage in team initiatives, supporting colleagues to achieve shared goals and deliver a seamless client experience.

**HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?**
At Cartier, we believe passion and expertise are the sparks that create extraordinary success.
  
  
**To Truly Thrive In This Role, You’ll Bring**

* Strong selling skills and an innate sense of service excellence, creating memorable client experiences that foster loyalty.
* Communicate fluently in Arabic and English.
* Leverage your product knowledge and genuine curiosity to engage clients and share the story behind each Cartier creation.
* Excel at time management and multitasking in a dynamic environment, adapting seamlessly to shifting priorities while maintaining meticulous attention to detail.
* Embrace a positive, team-oriented mindset, contributing to a supportive and inspiring boutique culture.

**What Do We Offer**
At Cartier, you will be part of a community bound by a strong sense of responsibility, where connection, collaboration, and collective spirit thrive.
  
  
We empower individual talents, united by a shared commitment to excellence, inspired by the world's diverse cultures, to respect and enrich our unique heritage. We believe that different perspectives fuel innovation and drive us to create exceptional experiences.
  
  
We offer an energizing environment for career growth and the opportunity to contribute your individual vision to a legacy of creativity.
  
  
We celebrate every achievement, big or small, fostering meaningful connections through gratitude and mutual support, nurturing a sense of belonging.
  
  
**Join us at Cartier, a place like no other, forever moving forward.**
**YOUR JOURNEY WITH US**
If your application is selected, you will receive an introductory call from a member of our Talent team to explain further steps and discuss the opportunity. If there is a match from both sides, you will have a chance to meet with the Boutique and Retail Management Team, as well as HR as part of a video or in-person interview. If we are positive from all sides and this is an exciting challenge for you, you will receive a job offer to join Cartier and a warm invitation to the team!
  
  
**Take your next step with Cartier, we look forward to hearing from you!**

data.jd_text:
**Job title:**
Supervisor, Operations

**Location:**
UAE

**Reporting to:**
Manager, Operations

**About noon**

We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to deliver to every door every day. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Supervisor, Operations who can help us move even faster.

noon’s mission: Every door, every day.

**What you'll do:**

Team noon has some of the fastest, smartest, and hardest-working people we've encountered. With a young, aggressive, and talented team, we're driving major missions forward.

The Supervisor will be responsible for managing end-to-end store operations, ensuring smooth day-to-day functioning, adherence to operational protocols, and delivery of high standards in inventory management, customer experience, and team performance.

* Own and manage the end-to-end store operations, ensuring efficiency, accuracy, and compliance with company standards.
* Ensure strict adherence to inventory management protocols, including:
* Maintaining 5S standards within the store
* Conducting weekly stock checks and daily inventory walks
* Performing regular security audits
* Lead and manage a team comprising shift leaders, logistics associates, delivery associates, warehouse staff, and cashiers to ensure optimal performance.
* Oversee payroll and attendance processes, ensuring timely closure as per defined timelines.
* Monitor and track key store performance metrics, identifying gaps and driving corrective actions.
* Ensure compliance with all applicable legal and local regulatory requirements.
* Conduct monthly store audits to ensure proper inventory placement and adherence to company policies.
* Own and drive key metrics related to inventory management, delivery experience, and customer satisfaction.
* Ensure all team members are adequately trained and equipped to perform their roles effectively.
* Take proactive actions during operational challenges and escalate issues in a timely manner when required.
* Manage store cash operations, including reconciliation and timely deposits.
* Plan and optimize workforce rosters for warehouse and delivery staff to ensure productivity and performance targets are met.
* Prepare and share daily reports on store performance and operational metrics.
* Identify opportunities to increase order volumes by improving serviceability and operational efficiency.

**What you'll need:**

* Bachelor’s degree in any relevant field.
* 3–6 years of relevant experience in operations, preferably in a supervisory or team leadership role.
* Industry experience in e-commerce, logistics, warehousing, or retail operations is mandatory.
* Strong understanding of Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets, with the ability to analyze and interpret data effectively.
* Good numerical and analytical skills, with strong attention to detail.
* Proven experience in team management and driving performance in a fast-paced environment.
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Ability to handle multiple priorities and work under pressure.
* Proactive problem-solving mindset with the ability to take ownership and drive results.

**Who will excel?**

* We’re looking for people with high standards, who understand that hard work matters.
* You need to be relentlessly resourceful and operate with a deep bias for action.
* We need people with the courage to be fiercely original.
* noon is not for everyone; readiness to adapt, pivot, and learn is essential.

data.raw_snippet:
